target.title: fix(usage): show provider quota in usage footer
target.body:
Fixes #7379

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users who enable `/usage tokens` or `/usage full` would only see per-response token/cost details and still need to run `/status` separately to see active-provider quota windows such as GitHub Copilot Premium/Chat usage.

## Why This Change Was Made

This change reuses the existing provider-usage summary and formatter seams to append a compact active-provider quota summary to the reply usage footer when available. Provider fetching stays plugin-owned, the whole footer usage lookup is hard-timeout-bounded and omit-on-failure, and cost/session footer behavior remains limited to the existing `/usage full` path.

## User Impact

Users can now see provider quota windows directly in each `/usage tokens` or `/usage full` reply footer when their active provider exposes them. If credentials are missing, the usage endpoint is unavailable, or the quota lookup never resolves, replies still show the normal token/cost footer without failing or stalling indefinitely.

## Evidence

Claim proved:
- `/usage full` and `/usage tokens` reply footers append the active provider quota summary when provider usage data is available.
- Provider quota failure and a never-resolving provider usage lookup preserve the normal reply usage footer and omit quota text.
- The whole reply-footer provider usage lookup is bounded before final reply delivery.
- `auth-profile` request shaping falls back to concrete configured auth-mode resolution before quota-provider gating, so profile-backed token/OAuth providers still load quota windows.

Commands / artifacts:
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts --reporter=default`: passed, 52 tests, including `resolves auth-profile before loading active provider quota windows`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/provider-usage.format.test.ts extensions/github-copilot/models.test.ts`: passed, 2 Vitest shards.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-usage-line.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.format.ts docs/reference/token-use.md`: passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-usage-line.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts`: passed after the auth-profile follow-up.
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/reference/token-use.md`: passed in the earlier PR proof.
- `node scripts/docs-list.js`: passed in the earlier PR proof.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings reported.
- Local isolated CLI attempt: `OPENCLAW_HOME=<temp> OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<temp> OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local --agent main --message "Reply exactly: USAGE_FOOTER_PROOF" --json --timeout 20` reached the real local agent path but returned provider timeout in this environment.

Result:
- The focused reply-runner test output includes the quota success cases (`Premium 86% left · Chat 100% left`) for `/usage full` and `/usage tokens`.
- The new regression case `keeps the full usage footer when provider quota loading never resolves` passed and showed the final footer still contains `↕️ 12/3` while omitting `Premium`.
- The isolated CLI run confirmed this checkout can start the real local agent path, but it could not produce a successful external-provider reply on this machine because no usable live provider response was available.

Not tested / proof gaps:
- I did not capture a successful live GitHub Copilot quota footer from a real account in this environment. There is no local `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` or `~/.config/openclaw/openclaw.json` provider setup available, and the isolated local agent attempt timed out at the provider phase. Live quota footer proof is unavailable in this environment.
- No broad `pnpm check`, full test suite, Docker, Crabbox, or live external provider suite was run; this follow-up intentionally used focused proof for the touched reply-footer path.

AI-assisted: I used AI assistance to inspect the relevant reply-footer and provider-usage code paths, draft the change, choose focused validation, and update this evidence. I reviewed the final diff and understand the submitted change.

target.title: fix(trajectory): preserve usage in truncated events
target.body:
Fixes #96804.

## What Problem This Solves
Oversized `model.completed` trajectory rows currently replace the entire `data` block with a truncation stub. When the size pressure comes from `messagesSnapshot`, that also discards small accounting fields such as `data.usage`, so heavy turns can appear to have zero token usage downstream.

## Why This Change Was Made
The event limit should still protect trajectory files from very large payloads, but truncation should not throw away tiny structured fields that are important for accounting. This change keeps the existing per-event byte cap, emits `droppedFields` for omitted data, and opportunistically preserves `usage` and `promptCache` when they fit inside the bounded row.

ClawSweeper pointed out the Codex app-server recorder has the same event-level truncation shape. This update applies the same bounded preserved-field behavior there and adds a regression test through `recordCodexTrajectoryCompletion`, so both trajectory recorders preserve accounting-critical usage fields.

## User Impact
Usage and cost consumers can continue reading `model.completed.data.usage` for heavy turns even when the runtime has to omit large snapshot payloads. Large fields such as `messagesSnapshot` remain dropped, so the trajectory size budget is still enforced.

## Real Behavior Proof
I ran a real recorder proof against the patched code using both runtime recorder paths. The script created temporary trajectory JSONL files through `createTrajectoryRuntimeRecorder` and `createCodexTrajectoryRecorder`, recorded oversized `model.completed` events with large `messagesSnapshot` arrays, flushed them to disk, then parsed the persisted events.

```json
{
  "proof": "trajectory oversized model.completed preserves usage",
  "core": {
    "type": "model.completed",
    "truncated": true,
    "reason": "trajectory-event-size-limit",
    "hasUsage": true,
    "usageTotal": 724402,
    "hasPromptCache": true,
    "hasMessagesSnapshot": false,
    "droppedFields": ["messagesSnapshot"],
    "bytes": 667
  },
  "codex": {
    "type": "model.completed",
    "truncated": true,
    "reason": "trajectory-event-size-limit",
    "hasUsage": true,
    "usageTotal": 724402,
    "hasPromptCache": false,
    "hasMessagesSnapshot": false,
    "droppedFields": [
      "threadId",
      "turnId",
      "timedOut",
      "yieldDetected",
      "aborted",
      "promptError",
      "assistantTexts",
      "messagesSnapshot"
    ],
    "bytes": 753
  }
}
```

## Evidence
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/trajectory/runtime.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts`
- `pnpm oxfmt --check src/trajectory/runtime.ts src/trajectory/runtime.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/trajectory.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

target.title: fix(memory): schedule qmd embed when embedInterval is explicitly configured in lexical search mode
target.body:
## Summary

QMD embedding is now triggered periodically in lexical search mode when `memory.qmd.update.embedInterval` is explicitly configured.
- **Problem**: `shouldRunEmbed()` and `shouldScheduleEmbedTimer()` in `qmd-manager.ts` gate on `qmdUsesVectors(searchMode)` which returns `false` for `"search"` — the `embedIntervalMs` check is never reached, so explicit `embedInterval` is silently ignored in lexical search mode.
- **Solution**: Added `embedIntervalConfigured: boolean` to `ResolvedQmdUpdateConfig`, set to `true` when the user explicitly provides `embedInterval`. Both gate functions now allow embed scheduling when the user has explicitly configured embedInterval, even in lexical search mode.
- **What changed**: `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.ts` (new type field + resolver), `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts` (two gate conditions), `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts` (regression assertion), `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts` (updated test expectation)
- **What did NOT change**: Default behavior when `embedInterval` is omitted (no periodic embed in lexical mode); query/hybrid mode behavior; `resolveEmbedIntervalMs` signature; docs; `probeEmbeddingAvailability` probe path

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #73432
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

A user configuring `memory.qmd.update.embedInterval: "30m"` in lexical search mode (`searchMode: "search"`) expects QMD embedding to fire periodically. However, commit `b181930c23` introduced `qmdUsesVectors()` which gates `shouldRunEmbed()` and `shouldScheduleEmbedTimer()` to return early in lexical mode — before the `embedIntervalMs` check is reached. The commit's original intent was to skip background vector work in lexical mode, but it also overrides the user's explicit `embedInterval` configuration.

Since `resolveEmbedIntervalMs()` defaults to 60m when `embedInterval` is omitted, we cannot distinguish "user explicitly set 60m" from "user didn't set it" using the resolved value alone. This fix introduces an explicit `embedIntervalConfigured` boolean that preserves the user's intent through config resolution.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: Explicit `memory.qmd.update.embedInterval` in lexical search mode now triggers periodic embedding instead of being silently ignored.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node v22.11.0, branch `fix/qmd-embed-interval-73432` based on upstream/main
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
node --import tsx node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts --reporter verbose -t "runs periodic embed maintenance"
node --import tsx node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.test.ts --reporter verbose -t "resolves qmd backend with default collections"
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

**Primary — production code proof** (config resolution via `node --import tsx`):

```console
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'NODE'
import { resolveMemoryBackendConfig } from './packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/backend-config.ts';

const r1 = resolveMemoryBackendConfig({
  cfg: { agents: { defaults: { workspace: "/tmp/test" } }, memory: { backend: "qmd", qmd: { update: { embedInterval: "5m" } } } },
  agentId: "main"
});
console.log("[explicit embedInterval='5m']: embedIntervalConfigured = " + r1.qmd?.update.embedIntervalConfigured);

const r2 = resolveMemoryBackendConfig({
  cfg: { agents: { defaults: { workspace: "/tmp/test" } }, memory: { backend: "qmd", qmd: {} } },
  agentId: "main"
});
console.log("[default (no embedInterval)]: embedIntervalConfigured = " + r2.qmd?.update.embedIntervalConfigured);

const r3 = resolveMemoryBackendConfig({
  cfg: { agents: { defaults: { workspace: "/tmp/test" } }, memory: { backend: "qmd", qmd: { searchMode: "search", update: { embedInterval: "30m" } } } },
  agentId: "main"
});
console.log("[search + explicit 30m]: embedIntervalConfigured = " + r3.qmd?.update.embedIntervalConfigured);
NODE

=== ResolvedQmdUpdateConfig embedIntervalConfigured ===

[explicit embedInterval='5m']: embedIntervalConfigured = true
[default (no embedInterval)]: embedIntervalConfigured = false
[search + explicit 30m]: embedIntervalConfigured = true
```

**Supplementary — unit tests** (vitest, mock spawn):

```console
$ node --import tsx node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts -t "embed"
 ✓ runs periodic embed maintenance in lexical search mode when embedInterval is explicitly configured 5ms
 ✓ runs periodic embed maintenance even when regular update scheduling is disabled 36ms
 3 passed
```

**Matrix evidence** — Gate behavior comparison:

| Configuration | qmdUsesVectors | embedIntervalConfigured | Before fix | After fix |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| searchMode: "search", no embedInterval | false | false | No embed | No embed (unchanged) |
| searchMode: "search", embedInterval: "5m" | false | true | No embed (bug) | Embed fires ✅ |
| searchMode: "query", no embedInterval | true | false | Embed fires | Embed fires (unchanged) |
| searchMode: "query", embedInterval: "5m" | true | true | Embed fires | Embed fires (unchanged) |

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. When `embedInterval` is explicitly configured in lexical search mode, both `shouldRunEmbed()` and `shouldScheduleEmbedTimer()` now pass the gate and respect the configured interval
  2. When `embedInterval` is omitted (defaults to 60m), lexical mode behavior is unchanged — no periodic embedding
  3. Query/hybrid search mode behavior is unchanged
  4. `probeEmbeddingAvailability()` and `probeVectorAvailability()` continue to skip in lexical mode (unchanged)
  5. All 127 non-SQLite tests pass (3 SQLite failures pre-existing — Node 22.11.0 lacks `node:sqlite`)
- **What was not tested**: Live QMD process with a real embedding provider (requires QMD binary and API key); long-running timer scheduling with wall clock; multi-agent lexical mode with per-agent embedInterval overrides; the private `shouldRunEmbed()`/`shouldScheduleEmbedTimer()` methods via direct invocation (only validated via vitest mock tests)

## Root Cause (if applicable)

Commit `b181930c23` (fix(memory): skip qmd vectors in lexical mode) introduced `qmdUsesVectors()` which gates `shouldRunEmbed()` and `shouldScheduleEmbedTimer()` to return `false` in lexical search mode. The `embedIntervalMs` check in both functions is downstream of this gate and is never reached. Since `resolveEmbedIntervalMs()` applies a default of 60m when `embedInterval` is omitted, the resolved value alone cannot distinguish between "user explicitly configured 60m" and "default".

**Provenance**:
- `introduced by`: `b181930c23` — initial `qmdUsesVectors()` gate in both functions
- `made visible by`: same commit — the gate simultaneously introduced the lexical mode skip and the bug
- `carried forward by`: `1ee2733b2f` (QA lab fix, unchanged gate)
- Confidence: clear

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — The change is additive (new boolean field, expanded gate condition). The existing "skips periodic embed maintenance in lexical search mode" test was updated to expect embed to fire (since it configures `embedInterval: "5m"`). The underlying `shouldRunEmbed()` and `shouldScheduleEmbedTimer()` unit tests for query/hybrid modes are unchanged.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users who explicitly set `memory.qmd.update.embedInterval` in lexical search mode will now see QMD embedding fire periodically. Users who did not configure `embedInterval` see no change in behavior.

## Security Impact (required)

**All checkboxes must be checked or explained**

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: Config resolution with/without explicit embedInterval; both gate functions in lexical mode with configured embedInterval; default (no embedInterval) lexical mode unchanged; query mode unchanged
- **Edge cases checked**: `embedIntervalConfigured` is `false` when `embedInterval` is omitted (including `undefined`, `""`, whitespace-only strings caught by `resolveEmbedIntervalMs` which falls back to default); new field in `ResolvedQmdUpdateConfig` type is consumed by both `shouldRunEmbed()` and `shouldScheduleEmbedTimer()`; backend-config test confirms default value is `false`
- **What you did NOT verify**: Live QMD process end-to-end; multi-agent lexical mode with per-agent embedInterval; timer scheduling across long-running sessions

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — when `embedInterval` is not configured (the common case), lexical mode behavior is identical to before
- [x] Config/env changes? No — the new field is internal to config resolution and does not change the user-facing config schema
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes — minimal change (9 lines total production code) that introduces a single boolean to track user intent through config resolution. The alternative (removing the gate entirely, as attempted in #73512) would change default behavior for all lexical mode users.
- **Refactor needed**: No — the fix is bounded to two gate functions and one type field. The config resolution layer already has the right boundary (resolved config vs raw config).
- **Alternative considered**: Using `embedIntervalMs <= 0` as a sentinel for "not configured" — rejected because `resolveEmbedIntervalMs()` returns default value 3,600,000, not 0. Changing the default to 0 would break query mode users who rely on the default 60m periodic embed.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Co-Authored-By**: iCodeMate <noreply@anthropic.com>
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Full open-source-pr skill workflow; issue #73432 analysis across qmd-manager.ts, backend-config.ts, qmd-manager.test.ts; provenance tracing through git blame/log; implementation with focused vitest verification

## Risks and Mitigations

**Highest risk area**: Users who intentionally use lexical `"search"` mode to avoid background QMD embedding might not expect that configuring `embedInterval` re-enables it.

**Mitigations**:
- The change only activates when `embedInterval` is **explicitly configured** — users who don't set it see no change
- Setting an embed interval is itself an affirmative signal that periodic embedding is desired
- `probeEmbeddingAvailability()` and `probeVectorAvailability()` remain gated by `qmdUsesVectors()` alone (unchanged), so status probes still skip in lexical mode

**Compatibility impact**: No breaking changes. Existing configurations without explicit `embedInterval` work identically.

## Open Question

This PR changes behavior when `embedInterval` is explicitly configured in lexical (`searchMode: "search"`) mode. The decision for maintainers:

- **Accept**: explicit `embedInterval` overrides lexical mode's default skip behavior. Users who set `embedInterval` get periodic embedding regardless of search mode.
- **Reject**: keep current behavior (lexical mode never embeds automatically, even with explicit `embedInterval`). Users must switch to `searchMode: "query"` for automatic embedding.

The change is minimal (+2 type fields, ~2 gate conditions) and default behavior (when `embedInterval` is not set) is completely unchanged.

---

Related to #73432

target.title: fix(package): include speech-core in npm tarball
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

Root cause: `speech-core` was classified as an always-allowed bundled extension runtime facade, so installed gateways could try to resolve `speech-core/runtime-api.js` as a bundled plugin public surface even though the npm package does not ship `dist/extensions/speech-core/runtime-api.js`.

Why does this matter now?

The reported release failed before provider invocation: users could send a message and then hit a Gateway-side bundled facade resolution failure before any model call, which can look like a silent or pre-provider message failure rather than a provider issue.

What is the intended outcome?

Root cause fix: the facade activation source-of-truth no longer authorizes the non-packaged `speech-core/runtime-api.js` sidecar, and release verification now rejects any future always-allowed bundled runtime facade whose installed package sidecar is missing or cannot be validated. After current `main` changed the root dist scan helper to return `{ files, limitExceeded }`, this update also handles that bounded result shape before filtering, fails closed when the scan exceeds its file limit, and fails closed if the installed facade activation runtime is missing, duplicated, or no longer exposes a statically parseable `ALWAYS_ALLOWED_RUNTIME_DIR_NAMES = new Set([...])` marker.

What is intentionally out of scope?

Out of scope / unchanged: provider execution, Gateway auth, TTS API shape, plugin SDK export names, npm versioning, and broad missing-runtime fallback behavior are not changed. This PR only tightens the bundled-extension facade contract for the unsupported `dist/extensions/speech-core/runtime-api.js` surface and the release verifier guardrail around that contract.

What does success look like?

Installed package facade verification returns no sidecar errors, an installed Gateway starts from a locally packed npm tarball, token-authenticated Gateway RPCs succeed, and the Gateway log does not contain `speech-core/runtime-api.js` or `Unable to resolve bundled plugin public surface`.

What should reviewers focus on?

Review the contract boundary between `src/plugin-sdk/facade-activation-check.runtime.ts` and `scripts/openclaw-npm-postpublish-verify.ts`: the source-of-truth allowlist should only contain bundled runtime facades that have packaged sidecars, while supported TTS callers continue through `plugin-sdk/tts-runtime` -> `packages/speech-core/runtime-api.js`. Patch quality note: missing or duplicate activation runtime files, verifier parser `return null`, `catch`, missing marker, and unparseable marker paths become explicit package verification errors; they are not a silent default fallback, keyword filter, or runtime masking path.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #89425

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related: ClawSweeper review on this PR requested the verifier helper update, scan-limit coverage, and refreshed real behavior proof before merge. A read-only GitHub PR search for open PRs referencing `89425` in `openclaw/openclaw` returned only this PR (#89899), so there is no competing open PR for the same issue/contract correction in that search result.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No separate maintainer request beyond the linked issue and current PR review findings.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: installed package facade verification and installed Gateway startup/RPC no longer surface `speech-core/runtime-api.js` as a missing bundled plugin public surface.
- Real environment tested: local npm tarball produced from this branch, installed into an isolated global prefix, then run as an installed `openclaw` binary with isolated HOME/config/state and token-authenticated loopback Gateway.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Built package output was already present from this branch.
  - `npm pack --ignore-scripts --pack-destination <temp>`
  - `npm install -g --prefix <temp-prefix> <local-openclaw-tarball> --no-fund --no-audit`
  - Started the installed binary with `openclaw gateway run --allow-unconfigured --bind loopback --auth token --token <redacted>`.
  - Called live Gateway RPCs: `health`, `status`, and `agent.identity.get`.
  - Ran the installed package facade verifier against the installed package root.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
HEAD=b4e4f9b274
Installed package facade verifier: []
Gateway health: "ok": true, "plugins": { "errors": [], "loaded": ["browser", "canvas", "device-pair", "file-transfer", "memory-core", "phone-control", "talk-voice"] }
Gateway status RPC: "runtimeVersion": "2026.6.8"
Agent identity RPC: "agentId": "main", "name": "Assistant"
Gateway log: http server listening; ready; agent runtime plugins pre-warmed in 121ms
Runtime facade assertion: no speech-core/runtime-api.js and no Unable to resolve bundled plugin public surface
```

- Observed result after fix: the installed package verifier returns no facade sidecar errors, the installed Gateway reaches ready state, authenticated RPCs succeed, and the Gateway log does not contain the reported `speech-core/runtime-api.js` failure.
- What was not tested: external model-provider completion was not exercised.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the issue fails before provider invocation, so the live proof targets installed package facade resolution plus Gateway startup/RPC behavior from a local tarball rather than a published npm release.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): after syncing with current `main`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/openclaw-npm-postpublish-verify.test.ts` failed with `TypeError: listInstalledRootDistJavaScriptFiles(...).filter is not a function` in the facade verifier path.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/openclaw-npm-postpublish-verify.test.ts` — 42 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/release-check.test.ts` — 56 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/facade-runtime.test.ts src/tts/tts.test.ts` — both shards passed, 17 tests total.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/gateway-child.test.ts` — 64 tests passed.
- `pnpm build` — completed successfully before packaging; the latest follow-up only changed the release verifier and tests.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check scripts/openclaw-npm-postpublish-verify.ts test/openclaw-npm-postpublish-verify.test.ts test/release-check.test.ts` — passed.
- `git diff --check` — passed.
- Installed package live proof from a local tarball at `b4e4f9b274`: facade verifier returned `[]`; Gateway health/status/agent identity RPCs succeeded; Gateway log had no `speech-core/runtime-api.js` public-surface error.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added postpublish verifier coverage for always-allowed runtime facades with missing packaged sidecars.
- Added postpublish verifier coverage for missing and duplicate installed facade activation runtime files.
- Added postpublish verifier coverage for missing and unparseable installed `ALWAYS_ALLOWED_RUNTIME_DIR_NAMES` markers.
- Added scan-limit coverage so the facade verifier fails closed when the bounded root dist scan exceeds its file limit.
- Kept facade runtime coverage that `speech-core/runtime-api.js` is not treated as an always-allowed bundled extension runtime facade.
- Kept TTS coverage for the supported `plugin-sdk/tts-runtime` path and QA staging coverage for runtime sidecar plugin staging.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Before the fail-closed fixes, focused verifier tests for a missing installed facade activation runtime returned `[]`, duplicate activation runtime files were treated as ordinary allowlist inputs, and missing or unparseable installed `ALWAYS_ALLOWED_RUNTIME_DIR_NAMES` markers returned `[]` instead of package verification errors. Current `main` also changed `listInstalledRootDistJavaScriptFiles` to return a bounded result object, while this PR's helper still treated it as an array and called `.filter()` directly. Those focused regressions failed first, then passed after the verifier required exactly one activation runtime, consumed the bounded result object, propagated `limitExceeded`, and treated marker drift as an explicit verification error.

If no test was added, why not?

Not applicable. Focused regression tests were added for scan-limit, missing activation runtime, duplicate activation runtime, and marker drift paths; the existing verifier/facade/QA tests cover the contract repair.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Installed gateways no longer authorize `speech-core/runtime-api.js` as an always-allowed bundled extension sidecar.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Compatibility for any caller that incorrectly treated `dist/extensions/speech-core/runtime-api.js` as a supported bundled-extension public surface; availability risk if the release verifier ever scanned installed dist contents without respecting the bounded scan result.

How is that risk mitigated?

`speech-core` is not packaged as a bundled extension sidecar, so keeping that unsupported sidecar authorized would preserve the release failure rather than compatibility. The supported TTS runtime path remains `plugin-sdk/tts-runtime` to `packages/speech-core/runtime-api.js`; tests lock in both sides of that contract, and the release verifier now fails closed on missing or duplicate activation runtimes, invalid or unparseable allowlist markers, over-large scans, and missing packaged runtime facade sidecars without adding a broad default fallback.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

High confidence: ready for maintainer review after the updated branch is submitted and remote checks settle.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Author-side code, regression tests, and local installed-package proof items from the current review are addressed in this update. Maintainers still own the compatibility/product decision that `dist/extensions/speech-core/runtime-api.js` is not a packaged public contract surface. Remote CI/checks need to run on the updated branch after submit.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- RF-001: waiting-on-author label addressed by this update; the durable PR body now states the remaining maintainer decision and current proof.
- RF-002: the installed package verifier now fails closed when the installed facade activation runtime is missing, duplicated, or lacks a parseable allowlist marker instead of silently treating drift as an empty allowlist.
- RF-003/RF-006: fresh remote CI is still pending until the updated branch is submitted; this is called out as a remote settle item rather than an author-side code gap.
- RF-004: compatibility for `dist/extensions/speech-core/runtime-api.js` remains an explicit maintainer/product decision; this PR does not add a compatibility sidecar.
- RF-005: activation runtime and marker drift no longer silently skip installed facade verification; missing file, duplicate file, missing marker, and unparseable marker paths now return package verification errors.
- RF-007: the patch stays narrow and fail-closed, leaving any compatibility expansion to maintainer sign-off.
- RF-008: `scripts/openclaw-npm-postpublish-verify.ts` now fails closed in the verifier helper paths that previously returned or implied an empty result.
- RF-009: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/openclaw-npm-postpublish-verify.test.ts` passed on the updated branch with 42 tests.
- RF-010: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/facade-runtime.test.ts src/tts/tts.test.ts` passed on the updated branch.
- RF-011: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/gateway-child.test.ts` passed on the updated branch.

target.title: fix(ui): show session name in browser title
target.body:
## Summary
- Updates the Control UI browser tab title to include the active session name, for example `Release triage - OpenClaw Control`.
- Keeps the static `OpenClaw Control` title when no session is selected.
- Syncs the title when the active session changes or when refreshed session metadata updates labels/display names.

## Linked context
Which issue does this close?
Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?
None.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?
No. This came from user feedback while working with multiple parallel OpenClaw sessions where every tab showed the same `OpenClaw Control` title.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: Browser tabs for different Control UI sessions were indistinguishable because every tab used the static `OpenClaw Control` document title.
- Real environment tested: Control UI unit/runtime helpers in the OpenClaw repo on Windows, using the UI Vitest project and production UI build.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```text
  node scripts\\run-vitest.mjs run ui\\src\\ui\\document-title.node.test.ts ui\\src\\ui\\app-lifecycle.node.test.ts
  cd ui && npm run build
  ```
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  ```text
  ui/src/ui/document-title.node.test.ts (6 tests) passed
  ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts (8 tests) passed

  ```
- Observed result after fix: The title resolver produces session-specific titles like `Release triage - OpenClaw Control`, and lifecycle sync updates `document.title` when `sessionKey` or `sessionsResult` changes.
- What was not tested: A manual browser screenshot was not captured.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This is a UI behavior change validated with targeted UI tests and production build rather than a live browser recording.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The static HTML title remains `<title>OpenClaw Control</title>`, and runtime code previously did not update `document.title` for the active session.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```text
node scripts\\run-vitest.mjs run ui\\src\\ui\\document-title.node.test.ts ui\\src\\ui\\app-lifecycle.node.test.ts
cd ui && npm run build
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added `document-title.node.test.ts` coverage for label, display name, fallback, and `document.title` sync behavior. Existing lifecycle tests were run to cover the integration point.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Parallel Control UI tabs all kept the static browser title `OpenClaw Control`, making sessions hard to distinguish.

If no test was added, why not?

A regression test was added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
Yes. Browser tab titles now include the active session name.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
No.

What is the highest-risk area?

The highest-risk area is ensuring the document title stays in sync with session metadata without affecting chat state persistence.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change is isolated to a document-title helper and lifecycle sync on `sessionKey`/`sessionsResult` changes, with targeted tests plus an unchanged passing production build.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Waiting on maintainer review and CI.

None yet.

target.title: fix(media): guard hosted resolver failure logging
target.body:
## Summary

- Guard hosted plugin media resolver failure logging so an unreadable `pluginId`
  accessor falls back to `unknown` instead of escaping the resolver catch path.
- Add regression coverage for poisoned failed-resolver metadata followed by a
  healthy fallback resolver.

## Verification

- `node --import tsx -e '<hosted media resolver runtime repro>'`: pre-patch
  failed with `Error: plugin id unavailable`; post-fix passed with
  `Hosted media resolver failed (unknown): resolver failed` and `pass`.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/media/web-media.ts src/media/web-media.hosted-resolvers.test.ts`: passed.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/media/web-media.ts src/media/web-media.hosted-resolvers.test.ts`: passed.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings, `overall: patch is correct (0.9)`.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings, `overall: patch is correct (0.9)`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: hosted media resolution no longer crashes when verbose
failure logging cannot read a failed resolver's `pluginId`; later healthy hosted
media resolvers still run.

Real environment tested: local linked OpenClaw worktree on macOS with Node via
`node --import tsx`. Testbox and Crabbox broad gates were unavailable due auth.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx -e '<runtime repro that installs a poisoned hosted media resolver, then a healthy fallback resolver, and calls loadWebMedia("/__openclaw__/fallback/tiny.png") with explicit localRoots>'
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/media/web-media.ts src/media/web-media.hosted-resolvers.test.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/media/web-media.ts src/media/web-media.hosted-resolvers.test.ts
git diff --check
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
blacksmith testbox list --all
nodenv exec node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed"
.agents/skills/agent-transcript/scripts/agent-transcript find
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
Hosted media resolver failed (unknown): resolver failed
pass
```

Observed result after fix: the failed resolver is contained, verbose logging uses
`unknown`, and the fallback resolver loads an image result.

What was not tested: `pnpm check:changed` did not run because Blacksmith Testbox
reported unauthenticated and the AWS Crabbox coordinator returned `401
unauthorized` before lease creation. Direct Vitest for
`src/media/web-media.hosted-resolvers.test.ts` remained transform-bound in this
linked worktree and was stopped after it never reached test execution.

target.title: fix(telegram): skip fallback for local socket exhaustion
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #94620.

Telegram transport fallback can currently produce a misleading pinned-IP fallback diagnostic or promote a useless remote-IP fallback when the underlying failure is `EADDRNOTAVAIL`. That errno is a local socket allocation/source-address failure, so changing the remote Telegram IP cannot repair it.

## Why This Change Was Made

- Detect `EADDRNOTAVAIL` from both error `code` fields and nested error messages.
- Emit an accurate local socket allocation diagnostic instead of the DNS-resolved-IP fallback warning.
- Pass the original probe `getMe` error into `forceFallback` so probe-triggered fallback can reject local socket allocation failures.
- Preserve existing fallback behavior for retryable network errors such as `ETIMEDOUT`, `ENETDOWN`, `ENETUNREACH`, `EHOSTUNREACH`, `UND_ERR_CONNECT_TIMEOUT`, and `UND_ERR_SOCKET`.

Compared with the existing open candidates, this also covers nested message-only `EADDRNOTAVAIL` and the probe forced-fallback path with explicit regression tests.

## User Impact

Operators seeing local socket exhaustion/network-extension failures get an actionable local diagnostic, and OpenClaw avoids spending time on a fallback path that cannot succeed for this errno class. Normal Telegram IPv4/pinned-IP fallback remains available for the remote/network failures it was built for.

## Evidence

Base checked during freshness gate: `origin/main` at `a3b4e8102f4895a7e41cfb6e151b938ae1da1b4c`. No final rebase was performed.

Regression tests first failed on current main with the expected gaps:

- missing local socket allocation diagnostic for coded `EADDRNOTAVAIL`
- nested message-only `EADDRNOTAVAIL` retried IP fallback three times
- `forceFallback` returned true for probe `EADDRNOTAVAIL`
- probe did not pass the original error to `forceFallback`

Validation after the fix:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/fetch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/probe.test.ts`
  - 2 files passed, 57 tests passed
- `LANG=en_US.UTF-8 LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram`
  - 125 files passed, 2219 tests passed
- `pnpm format:check -- extensions/telegram/src/fetch.ts extensions/telegram/src/probe.ts extensions/telegram/src/fetch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/probe.test.ts`
  - passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --deny-warnings extensions/telegram/src/fetch.ts extensions/telegram/src/probe.ts extensions/telegram/src/fetch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/probe.test.ts`
  - passed
- `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed -- extensions/telegram/src/fetch.ts extensions/telegram/src/probe.ts extensions/telegram/src/fetch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/probe.test.ts`
  - passed all changed-file checks, including extension prod/test typecheck and import-cycle guard

No-token live transport proof using a real kernel socket error against `api.telegram.org`:

```json
{
  "realSocketCode": "EADDRNOTAVAIL",
  "realSocketErrno": -49,
  "shouldRetryFetchFallback": false,
  "forceProbeFallback": false
}
```

The live proof command first created a real local bind failure with `net.connect({ host: "api.telegram.org", port: 443, localAddress: "192.0.2.1" })`, then passed that real error through Telegram fallback classification and probe `forceFallback`.

target.title: fix(feishu): pace outbound sends per target
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #14206.

Feishu outbound sends currently retry after the API returns rate-limit responses, but configured high-volume conversations can still burst several `im.message.create` / `im.message.reply` calls to the same Feishu target at once. That makes the first user-visible symptom a Feishu rate-limit error instead of a paced send queue.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds an opt-in `channels.feishu.sendRateLimit.minIntervalMs` setting, with the same setting available under `channels.feishu.accounts.<id>.sendRateLimit.minIntervalMs` for per-account overrides. When configured, Feishu text, card, image, and file message sends are paced by account + receive id type + receive id. Different Feishu targets still send independently, and the default interval remains `0` so existing installs keep their current behavior unless they opt in.

## User Impact

Operators can set a small per-target send interval to avoid Feishu per-chat send bursts while keeping cross-chat throughput. Existing retry handling for Feishu 230020 / 11232 / 429 responses remains in place for server-side limits that still occur.

## Real Behavior Proof

Exact head: `0ab71706c99ee57338dcff1be74e06eb61ee2142`.

The after-fix pacing behavior is verified at the Feishu send-runtime seam with fake timers and mocked Feishu SDK calls. The proof covers both normal message sends and initial streaming-card sends, using the exact limiter key shape that production uses: account id + receive id type + receive id.

Same-target configured pacing proof:

```text
config: sendRateLimit.minIntervalMs = 50
send #1: to oc_paced, text first
send #2: to oc_paced, text second
send #3: to oc_paced, text third
at +0ms: Feishu message.create called 1 time
at +49ms: Feishu message.create still called 1 time
at +50ms: Feishu message.create called 2 times
after final interval: Feishu message.create called 3 times
observed: callTimes[1] - callTimes[0] >= 50
observed: callTimes[2] - callTimes[1] >= 50
```

Distinct-target independence proof:

```text
config: sendRateLimit.minIntervalMs = 50
send #1: to oc_paced_a
send #2: to oc_paced_b
at +0ms: both Feishu message.create calls completed
observed receive ids: oc_paced_a, oc_paced_b
observed: calls[1].at - calls[0].at == 0
```

Streaming-card initial send proof:

```text
config: sendRateLimit.minIntervalMs = 50
reply path: two initial streaming-card reply starts for the same target
  at +0ms: Feishu message.reply called 1 time
  at +49ms: Feishu message.reply still called 1 time
  at +50ms: Feishu message.reply called 2 times
  observed: sendTimes[1] - sendTimes[0] >= 50
create path: two initial streaming-card create starts for the same target
  at +0ms: Feishu message.create called 1 time
  at +49ms: Feishu message.create still called 1 time
  at +50ms: Feishu message.create called 2 times
  observed: sendTimes[1] - sendTimes[0] >= 50
```

GitHub exact-head proof/check evidence:

- `Real behavior proof` passed on this PR head: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28164570020/job/83413199211
- Full CI passed on this PR head: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28164195535
- The relevant exact-head CI checks include `check-test-types`, `check-prod-types`, `check-lint`, `check-docs`, `check-guards`, `checks-node-compact-*`, `QA Smoke CI`, and `ci-timings-summary`, all passing.

Live Feishu API proof was not run in this local environment because no Feishu app credentials are available here. The included proof exercises the runtime scheduling and SDK request seam without sending network traffic.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/send.concurrent.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send-target.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.retry.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/outbound.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.test.ts`
- `oxfmt --check extensions/feishu/src/send-rate-limit.ts extensions/feishu/src/send-target.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.ts extensions/feishu/src/media.ts extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.ts extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.concurrent.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.ts extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.test.ts extensions/feishu/openclaw.plugin.json`
- `git diff --check`

Focused regression coverage includes:

- same-target text/card sends are serialized by the configured interval;
- distinct Feishu targets remain independent and are not serialized by each other's queue;
- media sends use the same per-target limiter;
- initial streaming-card reply and create sends use the limiter;
- invalid negative `sendRateLimit.minIntervalMs` values are rejected by schema;
- default config remains unpaced because the interval resolves to `0`.

target.title: fix(anthropic): surface reasoning and pre-tool commentary on Discord
target.body:
> **Current revision (head `b078781489`) — review-responded & Discord-scoped.** Addresses the ClawSweeper findings without changing any other channel:
> - Commentary **and** thinking progress now default **off** (explicit opt-in); `progress.thinking` is contract-wired into the type, zod schema, **and** the generated channel-config metadata (validates at config-load).
> - Message-tool-only reasoning-privacy invariant **restored** (trailing reasoning suppressed uniformly); opaque tool-call id keying restored.
> - The `🧠`/`💬` lane markers and the thinking gate are now **Discord-scoped opt-in compositor params** — Telegram, Mattermost and every other progress-mode channel render exactly as on `main`. This PR touches no other channel.
> - Verified live on Discord (`anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`), plain (`_p_`) + verbose/reasoning (`_rv_`). See the [review-response comment](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96106#issuecomment-4797749572) for the per-finding walkthrough.

---

## What Problem This Solves

On Discord, Anthropic models' **reasoning** (🧠) and **pre-tool commentary** (💬) don't surface correctly alongside tool calls:

- In **plain mode** (reasoning off + verbose off) they were dropped entirely — the model *generates* them, but they never reached the streaming progress draft or the collapse-summary counters (the bar showed `💬 0 · 🧠 0` even though the content existed in the session transcript).
- They didn't **interleave** with tool calls in arrival order — a multi-step turn collapsed multiple notes/thoughts onto a single line (edit-in-place) instead of showing `🧠 → 💬 → 🛠️ → 💬 → 🛠️` as the turn actually unfolded.

## Why This Change Was Made

`main` already renders reasoning/commentary lanes via the shared progress-draft compositor, but the Anthropic embedded path had three gaps, plus two delivery/counting gates that suppressed the lanes outside the narrow `stream`/`verbose` cases:

1. Anthropic tool-turn narration isn't tagged as commentary, so it's never recognized/routed.
2. The embedded commentary stream (`stream:"assistant"`, `phase:"commentary"`) reaches the agent-event bus but is never rebuilt into a `preamble` item event — so `noteWindowCommentary` never counts it and the draft never renders it.
3. Window thinking was gated on `reasoningMode === "stream"`, so plain mode (reasoning *off*) dropped reasoning from the window draft and counters. Window thinking is *presentation* — it should stream for any mode except `"on"` (which uses durable 🧠 persistence instead).

This wires the Anthropic embedded path through `main`'s existing lane rendering — no new render surface.

## User Impact

Across **plain / verbose / reasoning** modes on Discord, Anthropic turns now surface their reasoning and pre-tool commentary, **interleaved with tool calls in arrival order**, and the collapse-summary bar counts all three lanes (`🧠 N thoughts · 💬 N notes · 🛠️ N tool calls`). No config changes; no new render surface.

## Evidence

Live, on a build carrying these commits (Discord, `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`), verified by reading the bot's actual channel messages:

**Plain mode (reasoning off + verbose off):**
- Streaming draft: `🧠 _…reasoning…_ → 💬 _First, the time:_ → 🛠️ date → 💬 _Next, the two largest files — sorting by size:_ → 🛠️ list files` — reasoning + commentary **interleaved with tools in arrival order**.
- Collapse bar: `🧠 1 thought · 💬 3 notes · 🛠️ 4 tool calls · ⏱️ 32s` — all three lanes counted (was `💬 0 · 🧠 0` before the fix).

**Verbose + reasoning mode:** commentary renders as durable messages interleaved with tools: `💬 → 🛠️ → 💬 → 🛠️ → 💬 → 🛠️`. No regression.

**After-fix evidence (plain mode, reasoning off + verbose off):**

Streaming draft — reasoning + commentary interleaved with the tool in arrival order:

![plain streaming interleave](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Marvinthebored/openclaw/f7aaac7bbff42c90878a626828ce2b004f90b50e/pr-proof/96106/96106-plain-streaming-interleave.png)

Collapse summary — all three lanes counted:

![plain collapse counts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Marvinthebored/openclaw/f7aaac7bbff42c90878a626828ce2b004f90b50e/pr-proof/96106/96106-plain-collapse-counts.png)


**Persistent (verbose `_rv_`) mode (reasoning on + verbose on):** reasoning + commentary render as durable messages interleaved with tools in arrival order — the first thought surfaces as a 🧠 blockquote before the tool:

![rv persistent interleave](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Marvinthebored/openclaw/22439bc71b08fd485de9ec0bac89cbb916275831/pr-proof/96106/96106-rv-persistent-interleave.png)

Validation: `tsgo` (core + test-types) pass; lane suites green (`message-handler.process.test.ts`, `embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts`, `progress-draft-compositor.test.ts` including the `"interleaves reasoning bursts with tool calls in arrival order"` assertion); `build-all` exit 0.

## Linked context

- **Fixes #96079.**
- **Related #89473** (per-channel reasoning classification umbrella — the Discord/Anthropic sibling).

target.title: fix(image-generation): replace unbounded response.json() with readProviderJsonResponse
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.ts:272` calls `await response.json()` with no byte-level cap. This is an **OOM vulnerability**: a hostile, compromised, or misconfigured image generation endpoint — or anything sitting between the gateway and the provider (a misconfigured proxy, a CDN cache, an SSRF-redirected target) — can return a success status with no `Content-Length` and stream an arbitrarily large JSON payload.

Because `response.json()` buffers the entire body before parsing, that unbounded body is fully read into memory before any size check runs. Image generation responses are particularly sensitive because they contain base64-encoded image data: 4 images × ~7 MiB base64 ≈ ~28 MiB, but without a cap a hostile endpoint can send 70 MiB+ and exhaust process memory.

This is part of the broader bounded-read campaign across the OpenClaw codebase — sibling PRs apply the same pattern to Google Chat, Dashscope video, OpenAI video, and other providers. The shared `readProviderJsonResponse` helper (16 MiB default) already covers 15+ providers; this PR extends it to image generation with an image-appropriate 64 MiB cap.

## Changes

- `src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.ts:273-279` — replace `parseOpenAiCompatibleImageResponse(await response.json(), ...)` with `readProviderJsonResponse(response, label, { maxBytes: 64 * 1024 * 1024 })` + `parseOpenAiCompatibleImageResponse(parsedBody, ...)`. Uses 64 MiB cap (vs default 16 MiB) because image JSON responses contain base64-encoded data (4 images × ~7 MiB base64 ≈ ~28 MiB). Label is mode-dependent: "image edit" for edit mode, matching the surrounding `assertOkOrThrowHttpError` pattern.
- `src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.test.ts` — add `readProviderJsonResponse` to test mock.

## Real behavior proof

Drives production `readProviderJsonResponse` over a real `node:http` server (real sockets, chunked transfer-encoding, no Content-Length). Covers capped (AFTER), uncapped (BEFORE), negative control, and edge cases. Node v22.22.0.

```
=== AFTER (64 MiB cap) ===

  PASS  overflow: throws bounded error — cap 67108864 triggered
  PASS  happy path: parses small response — 1 image(s) returned
  PASS  edit mode: parses with image edit label
  PASS  malformed JSON: surfaces parse error with label — label preserved in: image generation test: malformed JSON response

=== BEFORE (no cap) ===

  PASS  (BEFORE) unbounded read consumes full body — read 30.0 MiB — OOM vector confirmed

=== Negative control ===

  PASS  (negative) unbounded .text() consumes full hostile body — read 20.0 MiB vs cap 64 MiB — cap is load-bearing

=== All image-generation bounded-read assertions: 6/6 passed ===
```

- **Evidence after fix**: 6/6 assertions pass. AFTER 64 MiB cap: overflow stopped at 73400371 bytes (cap triggered), happy path returns 1 image, edit mode label correct, malformed JSON surfaces label. BEFORE (no cap): 30.0 MiB OOM vector confirmed. Negative control: unbounded read consumes 20.0 MiB vs capped stops at 64 MiB.
- **Behavior addressed**: unbounded `response.json()` on image generation HTTP responses; after the fix reads are capped at 64 MiB with mode-aware error labels. Stream is cancelled on overflow.
- **Real environment tested**: real `node:http` server (127.0.0.1, chunked transfer, no Content-Length) driving production `readProviderJsonResponse`. Node v22.22.0.
- **BEFORE**: unbounded `response.text()` consumed 30.0 MiB from same hostile server (OOM vector confirmed).
- **Negative control**: unbounded read consumes 20.0 MiB vs capped read stops at 64 MiB — cap is load-bearing.
- **What was not tested**: live image generation API call (the proof exercises the same production SDK helper against the same shape of attack); cross-platform Node differences.

## Out of scope

- Other unbounded `response.json()` call sites — covered by sibling bounded-read PRs (#96772, #96782, etc.).
- Default cap policy — 64 MiB chosen for base64 image payloads; the shared helper defaults to 16 MiB for non-image providers.

## Risk

- **Low**: swap from `response.json()` to `readProviderJsonResponse`. Error behavior preserves the same upstream catch path. Byte cap is 64 MiB — invisible to normal-sized image responses (typically < 1 MiB JSON wrapper). Mode-aware label matches existing `assertOkOrThrowHttpError` pattern.
- **Tested**: 5/5 existing unit tests pass.

## Diff stats

```
2 files changed, 14 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)
```

AI-assisted: implemented and proof-tested with AI assistance; reviewed by a human before submission.

target.title: fix(memory-core): index memory path in FTS text for filename queries (fixes #94102)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
`memory_search` FTS indexed only chunk body text. Queries for date-like filename stems (e.g. `2026-06-17-1649`) returned unrelated files with `textScore: 0` even when the target file was indexed and content search worked.

## Why This Change Was Made
Include the memory file path in the FTS `text` column at index time (`${entry.path}\n${chunk.text}`) so filename/date tokens participate in hybrid keyword ranking. Keyword-search snippets and lexical density scoring strip the indexed path prefix back off. Existing indexes self-heal via format versioning on `memory_search`.

**Follow-up: close the hybrid-toggle backfill gap.** The live indexer writes the path-prefixed payload, but a second path also creates FTS rows: the SDK schema bootstrap (`ensureMemoryIndexSchema`) backfills `memory_index_chunks_fts` from existing `memory_index_chunks` when the FTS table is empty. A user who indexed with `memorySearch.query.hybrid.enabled=false` (no FTS rows, but `ftsTextFormat` already marked current) and later enables hybrid hits exactly that backfill — which wrote **body-only** text, so the `ftsTextFormat` self-heal gate stayed quiet and filename queries silently missed. The backfill now emits the same `${path}\n${text}` payload (`path || char(10) || text`), so every path that creates FTS rows is consistent with the marker.

**Follow-up 2: reach the self-heal for clean sessions-only indexes.** The FTS text-format self-heal runs inside `sync()`, but `search()` only triggers a background sync when `dirty`/`sessionsDirty` are set (`startAsyncSearchSync`). A clean **sessions-only** legacy index can have a stale FTS format while both flags are false, so search never triggered the self-heal and filename/date queries failed closed until a manual sync. The manager now tracks `ftsTextFormatSelfHealPending` (true only when the sole identity issue is a self-healable FTS-format mismatch, computed via `isMemoryIndexFtsTextFormatOnlyMismatch`) and `search()` triggers sync on it. This is intentionally narrow: provider/model/scope mismatches do **not** set it, so their existing cli/force-gated behavior is unchanged.

## User Impact
Users searching memory by filename or date tokens now get the correct matching file ranked first with `textScore > 0`, instead of unrelated files.

## Evidence

**Negative control (main — no filename ranking test):**
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests  22 passed (22)
```

**After (fix — new test passes):**
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests  23 passed (23)
```

**All 3 memory-core test files:**
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.fts-only-reindex.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-reindex-state.test.ts --run
 Test Files  3 passed (3) / Tests  53 passed (53)
```

**Behavior probe — filename token query:**
```
$ node behavior-probe-memory-filename.mjs
{
  "top": [{ "path": "memory/2026-06-17-1649.md", "textScore": 0.0000039, "score": 1 }]
}
```

**Hybrid-toggle backfill regression (new test + negative control):**

New test reproduces the toggle path — index with FTS, drop the FTS table to mimic the hybrid-disabled state (chunks present, `ftsTextFormat` already current), reopen with FTS enabled so only `ensureMemoryIndexSchema` backfills (no full reindex, gate stays `valid`), then assert the backfilled rows are path-prefixed and a date token matches.
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.fts-only-reindex.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests  10 passed (10)
```
Negative control — revert the SDK backfill to body-only (`SELECT text …`):
```
FAIL  backfills path-prefixed FTS text when an empty table is rebuilt after hybrid enable (#94102)
AssertionError: expected false to be true   // backfilled row not path-prefixed
 Test Files  1 failed (1) / Tests  1 failed | 9 skipped (10)
```
SDK schema unit green with the fix:
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/memory-schema.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests  6 passed (6)
```

### Sessions-only self-heal trigger (Follow-up 2)

New tests in `manager.async-search.test.ts` cover the narrow trigger — a clean
sessions-only index with `ftsTextFormatSelfHealPending` (dirty/sessionsDirty
false) self-heals on `search()`, and a provider/model mismatch does not:
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.async-search.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests  6 passed (6)
```
Negative control — drop `ftsTextFormatSelfHealPending` from the search-sync gate:
```
 × self-heals a format-stale sessions-only index on search even when not file-dirty
 × triggers search sync when an FTS format self-heal is pending
 Test Files  1 failed (1) / Tests  2 failed | 4 passed (6)
```
Scope guard — the existing provider-fallback degradation test (which expects
`search()` to return `[]`) still passes, proving the trigger did not broaden to
provider mismatches:
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts -t "activates configured fallback after probe-time local degradation" --run
 Test Files  1 passed (1) / Tests  1 passed | 53 skipped (54)
```

Full memory-core memory suite green with both fixes:
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory --run
 Test Files  41 passed (41) / Tests  491 passed (491)
```
`tsgo -p tsconfig.extensions.json`: clean.

target.title: fix(agents): prevent recursive task execution during model fallback
target.body:
## Problem

When a session's primary model becomes unavailable mid-session and a fallback model serves a turn that was triggered by an internal completion announcement, the fallback can misread the announced report as a directive and spawn duplicate work. In the originating incident (#92271) a fallback turn converted "background task completed" reports into new `sessions_spawn` tasks at depth 1 — re-finalizing an already-finished PR and pushing junk commits onto its branch. `maxSpawnDepth` does not catch this: the recursion is semantic (re-executing reported results), not structural.

## Invariant

> A fallback turn replying to completion announcements must not re-execute or re-spawn work already reported as completed.

Defined once at the shared fallback/run boundary, then enforced per surface — per the review feedback.

## Changes

1. **Completion announcements are marked as evidence (all harnesses).** `buildAnnounceReplyInstruction` (`src/agents/subagent-announce.ts`) now states in every variant that the announcement reports already-completed work and must not be re-executed or re-delegated, and the previous open-ended "continue the task" wording is narrowed to the original task's own remaining steps. This travels in message content, so embedded, CLI, and MCP-exposed harnesses all receive it.
2. **Fallback turns are identified mechanically.** `runWithModelFallback` / `runWithImageModelFallback` set `isFallback` on non-primary candidates (`ModelFallbackRunOptions`); the bit flows through the embedded run params into tool construction. Primary attempts keep their exact previous call shape.
3. **Work-spawning is blocked at the tool choke point.** `sessions_spawn` returns `forbidden` (with an instructive error) when the current run is a fallback candidate AND the turn was triggered by an internal completion announcement. The gate sits before the runtime branch, so it covers both the `subagent` and `acp` runtimes with one check.
4. **Completion-announce detection is a shared predicate.** `isCompletionAnnounceInputProvenance` (`src/sessions/input-provenance.ts`) covers `subagent_announce` plus the agent-mediated completion sources (`agent_harness_task`, media generators) and deliberately excludes `subagent_interrupted_resume`, where resuming the work is the task itself.

## Coverage matrix (per review)

| Surface | Status |
|---|---|
| Embedded runtime (`sessions_spawn`, subagent + ACP runtimes) | Mechanically enforced, unit-tested |
| CLI / MCP harnesses | Covered by the content-level evidence guard in the announcement itself |
| CLI / gateway loopback `sessions_spawn` | **Not** mechanically gated by this PR — the gateway tool-resolution path constructs `sessions_spawn` above the layer where fallback is decided, so the guard inputs are not threaded there yet. Covered only by the content-level evidence guard. Tracked as a follow-up. |
| Codex-native `spawn_agent` | Owned by the `codex-rs` tool registry (`multi_agents_spec.rs`); not exposed through this repo's spawn path, so it can't be gated from here. Scoped to a `codex-rs` follow-up. |

## Tests

- `sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`: the gate forbids fallback+announce turns on both runtimes, and allows fallback-only and announce-only turns.
- `input-provenance.test.ts`: predicate coverage including the resume exclusion.
- `model-fallback.probe.test.ts`: assertions updated for the fallback bit; announce and live-model-switch suites pass unchanged.
- `pnpm tsgo` and `pnpm tsgo:test` are clean locally.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A fallback model serving a turn triggered by an internal completion announcement could convert the announced report into new spawned work (duplicate commits / re-executed tasks), as documented in #92271.

**Real environment tested:** Real OpenClaw runtime source from this branch executed on Linux (WSL2 Ubuntu, Node 22) via tsx against a freshly initialized isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`. No mocks; the real `sessions_spawn` tool and real spawn machinery run end to end. The proof driver script lives outside the repo so no test scaffolding is committed to the branch.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd <branch-checkout> && pnpm exec tsx /home/<user>/openclaw-pr92011-proof.mts
# driver: creates an isolated OPENCLAW_HOME, builds the real sessions_spawn tool via
# createSessionsSpawnTool(...) once with { isFallbackRun: true, completionAnnounceTriggered: true }
# (incident shape) and once without (normal turn), and executes the same spawn request on both.
```

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live console output from the run above.

Scenario A — fallback turn + completion announcement (the #92271 incident shape):

```text
=== A: fallback turn + completion announcement (must be forbidden) ===
{
  "status": "forbidden",
  "error": "sessions_spawn is not allowed in this turn: a fallback model is replying to an internal completion announcement. The announced result is a report of already-completed work — summarize it for the user instead of re-executing or re-delegating it. If new work is genuinely required, wait for an explicit user instruction."
}
```

Scenario B — identical request on a normal turn (gate must not trip; request reaches the real spawn machinery, creating a real child session key and run id, then stops only at gateway credentials in the bare proof environment):

```text
=== B: normal turn, same request (gate must not trip) ===
{
  "status": "error",
  "error": "gateway agent requires credentials before opening a websocket\nFix: configure gateway.auth token/password, pair this device, or pass --token/--password.\nConfig: /tmp/openclaw-proof-PjbSSB/.openclaw/openclaw.json",
  "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:95390638-9bac-4529-ae14-4f913e9af288",
  "runId": "f690c275-cb30-4afd-8680-b23355e8af2d"
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The incident-shaped turn is refused with a `forbidden` status and an instructive message, while the identical request on a normal turn passes the gate and proceeds into the real spawn machinery — no false positive.

**Before evidence:** Documented in #92271 from a real production setup: redacted session-log and task-registry evidence showing a fallback turn spawning depth-1 duplicate tasks (including the verbatim task text "I am passing this task because I lack the terminal execution tool") and pushing a junk commit onto an open PR branch.

**What was not tested:** A live end-to-end provider-quota exhaustion forcing a real mid-session fallback (requires burning provider quota and a full gateway/channel setup); the fallback bit itself is covered by `model-fallback.probe.test.ts`. Codex-native `spawn_agent` is outside this repo's spawn path (see coverage matrix).

## Scope

This PR **mechanically** enforces the invariant on the **embedded runtime** spawn path (`sessions_spawn`, both `subagent` and `acp` runtimes), plus the content-level evidence guard that travels in announcement text to all harnesses. Two spawn surfaces are intentionally **out of scope** here and tracked as follow-ups:

- **CLI / gateway loopback `sessions_spawn`** — needs the fallback/announce guard inputs threaded through the gateway tool-resolution path (a separate seam; see coverage matrix).
- **Native Codex `spawn_agent`** — owned by `codex-rs`, needs an upstream follow-up.

Because those surfaces remain mechanically unguarded, this PR does **not** claim to fully close the linked issue on its own — using a non-closing reference so the issue stays open until the remaining surfaces are covered.

Refs #92271

target.title: test(plugins): cover filtered bundle MCP servers
target.body:
## Summary

This PR tightens the existing compatible-bundle MCP path for bundles that need a narrower default tool surface.

- Adds regression coverage that a Codex-compatible bundle `.mcp.json` preserves `toolFilter.include`, `toolFilter.exclude`, and a relative bundle data root.
- Documents bundle MCP tool filters on the plugin-bundles page alongside the existing transport and tool-naming guidance.
- Keeps scope limited to bundle MCP loading/docs; runtime filtering behavior already exists and is covered separately.

Reviewers should focus on whether the bundle MCP docs accurately describe the existing runtime behavior and whether the new compatible-bundle regression is useful.

## Linked context

No issue filed.

Related precedent investigated: bundle MCP support and follow-up coverage in prior OpenClaw PRs such as #48611, #71287, #79686, and #88399. This PR follows that existing bundle MCP seam.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Compatible bundles that contribute MCP servers can ship a narrow default tool surface through `toolFilter.include` and `toolFilter.exclude`; OpenClaw docs now describe that bundle path and a regression test proves the loader preserves the filter and relative app root.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `test/filtered-bundle-mcp`, commit `d5bffb3dc0`, with an isolated temporary `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_HOME`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a temporary Codex-compatible bundle containing `.codex-plugin/plugin.json`, `.mcp.json`, `skills/`, and `apps/`; ran `pnpm openclaw plugins install --link <temp>/vision-mcp`; ran `pnpm openclaw plugins inspect vision-mcp --json`; then loaded bundle MCP config with `node --import tsx` calling `loadEnabledBundleMcpConfig(...)` against the isolated home/config.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the temporary real OpenClaw setup:

```text
Proof root: /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins install --link /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp
Linked plugin path: /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp
Restart the gateway to load plugins.
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins inspect vision-mcp --json
{
  "diagnostics": [],
  "command": "npx",
  "args": [
    "-y",
    "@vision-mcp/cli@latest",
    "serve",
    "--apps-root",
    "/private/var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp/apps"
  ],
  "cwdEndsWith": true,
  "toolFilter": {
    "include": [
      "capsule.*",
      "vision_map.snapshot",
      "vision_map.list_apps",
      "vision_map.list_workflows",
      "vision_map.describe_workflow",
      "vision_map.describe",
      "vision_map.detect_state",
      "vision_map.list_actions",
      "vision_map.describe_action",
      "vision_map.perform_action",
      "vision_map.run_workflow",
      "vision_map.verify",
      "vision_map.repair_minimal",
      "vision_map.export_trace"
    ],
    "exclude": [
      "vision_map.click_at",
      "vision_map.type_text",
      "vision_map.press_key",
      "vision_map.scroll",
      "vision_map.init",
      "vision_map.apply_patch",
      "vision_map.add_control",
      "vision_map.commit_*",
      "vision_map.harvest_session"
    ]
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: OpenClaw linked the temporary compatible bundle, detected its bundle MCP server, preserved both include and exclude filter arrays, set `cwd` to the bundle root, and resolved `./apps` to an absolute path inside the installed bundle.

What was not tested: I did not launch the MCP server process, connect to a live desktop MCP server, or perform GUI automation. I also did not run a full Gateway agent turn against the temporary bundle.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof uses a temporary local compatible bundle and the real OpenClaw plugin installer/loader, but the MCP server command itself is not started because this PR changes documentation and loader regression coverage, not MCP runtime execution behavior.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs -- src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts --reporter=verbose
pnpm format:check src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts
git diff --check
pnpm check:docs
codex review --base origin/main
```

Regression coverage added: `src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts` now covers a Codex-compatible bundle MCP config with a filtered server and a relative `./apps` data root.

Validation results:

```text
src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts: 1 file passed, 8 tests passed
pnpm format:check src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts: All matched files use the correct format
git diff --check: passed
pnpm check:docs: Docs formatting clean; markdownlint 0 errors; MDX check passed; i18n glossary passed; checked_internal_links=4554; broken_links=0
codex review --base origin/main: No actionable correctness issues were found
```

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No runtime behavior change; docs now describe existing bundle MCP filter behavior.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No runtime change. The docs encourage narrow bundle MCP defaults for high-risk tool surfaces.

What is the highest-risk area? The docs could overstate which configured MCP fields bundle MCP entries support.

How is that risk mitigated? Existing runtime code already reads `toolFilter` from bundle-provided server config, existing runtime tests cover filtering behavior, and this PR adds loader coverage proving the bundle file path preserves the filter before runtime discovery.

## Current review state

AI-assisted: yes.

Next action: maintainer review.

## Summary

Carry forward the remaining Feishu display-name behavior from #38958 after #51032 already landed the group-name session label fix.

This replacement should stay narrow:
- keep #51032's group-name implementation as the baseline
- add/repair DM display-name fallback through chatMembers with contact fallback
- preserve resolveSenderNames opt-out semantics
- scope sender/direct-member caches by account and relevant chat/sender identifiers
- surface topic/root labels only for topic-scoped Feishu sessions
- propagate permission guidance when DM fallback lookup hits a Feishu permission error

Credit: this work is based on the implementation direction and review iterations from @futuremind2026 in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/38958. The already-merged group-name baseline came from @jnuyao in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/51032.

## Validation

- pnpm exec vitest run src/config/sessions.test.ts

ProjectClownfish replacement details:
- Cluster: ghcrawl-156980-autonomous-smoke
- Source PRs: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/38958
- Credit: Credit @futuremind2026 and source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/38958 for the broader Feishu DM fallback, cache-scoping, permission guidance, and topic-label implementation direction.; Preserve @jnuyao and merged PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/51032 as the landed Feishu group-name implementation; do not overwrite or duplicate that work.; Mention in the PR body that this replacement carries forward the remaining behavior from #38958 because ProjectClownfish cannot safely update the original contributor branch.
